chapter_title.ar: حَدِيثُ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ (عليه السلام) مَعَ يَزِيدَ
chapter_title.en: Hadith of Ali Bin Al-Husayn<sup>asws</sup> with Yazeed<sup>la</sup>

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6 / 260)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6 / 260)
text: دَخَلْتُ عَلَى أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام) فَقَالَ مَا لِي أَرَاكَ سَاهِمَ الْوَجْهِ فَقُلْتُ إِنَّ بِي حُمَّى الرِّبْعِ فَقَالَ مَا [ذَا] يَمْنَعُكَ مِنَ الْمُبَارَكِ الطَّيِّبِ اسْحَقِ السُّكَّرَ ثُمَّ امْخُضْهُ بِالْمَاءِ وَ اشْرَبْهُ عَلَى الرِّيقِ وَ عِنْدَ الْمَسَاءِ قَالَ فَفَعَلْتُ فَمَا عَادَتْ إِلَيَّ.

en.hubeali: H 14832 – Muhammad Bin yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Isa, from Ali Bin Al-Hakam, from Kamil Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Ibrahim Al-Ju’fy who said:

My father narrated to me that he came up to Abu Abdullahasws whoasws said; ‘Why is it that Iasws see you with a grim face?’ So I said, ‘I have fever’. So heasws said: ‘What prevents you from the blessed and good. Take the sugar and dissolve it in the water and drink it on an empty stomach and during the evening’. He said, ‘I did that, so it (fever) did not return to me’.
en.sarwar: 385. Muhammad Bin yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Isa, from Ali Bin Al-Hakam, from Kamil Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Ibrahim Al-Ju’fy who said: My father narrated to me that he came up to Abu Abdullah (a.s.) who (a.s.) said; ‘Why is it that I (a.s.) see you with a grim face?’ So I said, ‘I have fever’. So he (a.s.) said: ‘What prevents you from the blessed and good. Take the sugar and dissolve it in the water and drink it on an empty stomach and during the evening’. He said, ‘I did that, so it (fever) did not return to me’.
